requirements.statement: The application shall create a workspace in a user-selected local directory without requiring an account or network connection.
requirements.acceptance: A new Airgap installation creates, closes, and reopens a workspace while all network access is denied.

requirements.statement: The workspace shall declare a stable workspace ID, schema version, enabled modules, locale, and profile in a readable manifest.
requirements.acceptance: The manifest validates against the published schema and can be inspected in a text editor.

requirements.statement: Canonical Markdown, YAML, and JSONL shall remain sufficient to rebuild every disposable projection.
requirements.acceptance: Deleting the projection directory and running rebuild preserves all current semantic records and produces a reconciliation report.

requirements.statement: Canonical writes shall use revision preconditions and a crash-recoverable journal.
requirements.acceptance: An interrupted write leaves either the old valid revision or a recoverable journal; validation never reports silent loss.

requirements.statement: Unknown extension fields and body sections shall survive a read-modify-write cycle.
requirements.acceptance: A fixture containing unknown namespaced fields and sections is edited through the CLI and compares equal for all unknown content.

requirements.statement: The application shall detect duplicate record IDs, invalid schemas, revision regressions, and unsafe paths.
requirements.acceptance: Validation reports each defect with file, record ID, severity, and recovery guidance without deleting the source file.

requirements.statement: Migrations shall support dry-run, pre-migration backup, deterministic execution, validation, and rollback or an explicit irreversibility statement.
requirements.acceptance: The migration test runs twice with the same result and restores the pre-migration workspace from its backup.

requirements.statement: The desktop application shall display the active workspace, build profile, save state, projection state, and validation state.
requirements.acceptance: Each state is available visually and through screen-reader semantics without relying on colour alone.

requirements.statement: A person shall have a stable ID independent of filename, display name, contact address, or provider UID.
requirements.acceptance: Renaming the file and changing all contact points preserves person identity and references.

requirements.statement: A profile shall support structured names, aliases, pronouns, multiple typed contact points, addresses, URLs, and messaging handles.
requirements.acceptance: Round-trip tests preserve order, type labels, preferred flags, unknown vCard fields, and Unicode content.

requirements.statement: A profile shall support birthdays with an unknown year, anniversaries, and user-defined important dates.
requirements.acceptance: Date calculations and exports do not invent an age when the year is unknown.

requirements.statement: Custom profile fields shall use stable field IDs, declared types, validation, classification, and display policy.
requirements.acceptance: Renaming a field label does not orphan values or change its stable ID.

requirements.statement: Sensitive profile fields shall record provenance, verification date, visibility, and last-updated information.
requirements.acceptance: A field detail view identifies source and age without exposing the value to an unauthorised role.

requirements.statement: Archive shall remove a person from active workflows without deleting the canonical record or history.
requirements.acceptance: Archive and restore preserve ID, links, interactions, events, and provenance.

requirements.statement: Users shall create, archive, safely rehome, reorder, and change visibility of typed custom fields and tabs through stable IDs without changing canonical field meaning or hiding canonical content from non-visual access.
requirements.acceptance: A0 supports two saved layouts, keyboard and screen-reader operation, settings diff and rollback, and export or import while field IDs, values, unknown Markdown, validation, CLI, and export semantics remain unchanged.

requirements.statement: Dietary information shall distinguish allergy, intolerance, medical restriction, religious restriction, ethical preference, dislike, positive preference, and other.
requirements.acceptance: Each kind validates independently and can produce an authorised operational instruction.

requirements.statement: Dietary source facts shall store availability, freshness, conflict, and disclosure as four orthogonal axes; legacy mutually exclusive coverage values are migration inputs only.
requirements.acceptance: Every supported combination is validated without collapsing axes, an absent field is unknown rather than none, and migration preserves the legacy source value and provenance.

requirements.statement: Dietary operational instructions and detailed notes shall use separate classifications and disclosure policies; B0 recipients receive immutable least-disclosure brief bytes and never receive workspace access or a catering-role grant.
requirements.acceptance: The trusted B0 owner can preview and emit the operational instruction while detailed notes remain structurally absent; a recipient cannot open, query, or infer the workspace record.

requirements.statement: The product shall model organisations, locations, departments, teams, cost centres, and roles as stable records.
requirements.acceptance: People can be filtered by each dimension without encoding it as an unvalidated free-text tag.

requirements.statement: Memberships shall record effective dates and provenance.
requirements.acceptance: A person can move departments while historical event and interaction reports retain the applicable membership snapshot.

requirements.statement: Saved cohorts shall combine organisation, location, department, team, cost centre, role, group, and profile predicates.
requirements.acceptance: The cohort shows its active predicates and can be reproduced at a later time against named profile revisions.

requirements.statement: Bulk employee import shall provide mapping, validation, duplicate detection, preview, idempotency, and rollback evidence.
requirements.acceptance: Re-importing the same source does not duplicate people or memberships and produces a reconciliation report.

requirements.statement: Relationships shall support typed person-person and person-organisation links with direction, status, priority, cadence, and provenance.
requirements.acceptance: Changing a relationship priority does not modify either person profile.

requirements.statement: Relationship status shall support at least VIP, maintain, watch, deprioritised, ignored, and archived through configurable vocabulary.
requirements.acceptance: Views and reminders can filter by status while retaining stable machine values after label customisation.

requirements.statement: The product shall display last interaction, last meaningful note, next action, and due cadence for a relationship.
requirements.acceptance: Each summary links to its source and labels imported coverage limitations.

requirements.statement: The graph shall not be the only relationship interface.
requirements.acceptance: Every graph node, edge, filter, and supported action is available in a keyboard-operable semantic table or tree.

requirements.statement: Graph layout shall support bounded neighbourhoods, clustering, filtering, and progressive loading.
requirements.acceptance: A 100,000-person synthetic workspace does not attempt to render every node at once.

requirements.statement: The unified timeline shall include source-linked notes, interactions, meetings, and events with direction, channel, participants, time, kind, source and date filters, expandable content, correction history, and explicit imported coverage or range; factual counts shall never become a global person score.
requirements.acceptance: A local timeline works before providers, a bounded imported source names its requested and covered range plus skips, corrections and completeness limits, and no file, CLI, UI, projection, export, API, plugin, or automation output derives a person score from activity counts.

requirements.statement: Imported calendar and email metadata shall retain provider identities and support idempotent refresh.
requirements.acceptance: Refreshing an unchanged source creates no duplicate interaction.

requirements.statement: Email bodies and attachments shall not be imported under the default email-metadata grant.
requirements.acceptance: Network fixtures containing bodies and attachments result only in the explicitly granted metadata fields.

requirements.statement: Communication counts and last-interaction summaries shall identify their source, time range, and completeness limits.
requirements.acceptance: A partial mailbox import is not labelled as lifetime total.

requirements.statement: The product shall support source-linked meeting preparation views for authorised executive assistants.
requirements.acceptance: A briefing links every derived statement to a profile field, interaction, event, or explicit operator note.

requirements.statement: Users shall create events and add attendees directly, by import, or by saved cohort.
requirements.acceptance: The event records selection method, source, and attendee revision snapshot.

requirements.statement: Attendance shall distinguish invited, confirmed, attended, declined, cancelled, no-show, and unknown.
requirements.acceptance: Participation counts use recorded states and expose their definition.

requirements.statement: Dietary readiness shall account for every selected attendee and each defined coverage state.
requirements.acceptance: The category counts plus unresolved identities reconcile exactly to the selected cohort.

requirements.statement: A catering brief shall be an immutable purpose-bound least-disclosure snapshot for a named recipient or recipient class; names shall be absent by default and an opaque attendee identifier shall appear only when the approved policy explicitly requires one.
requirements.acceptance: Preview bytes and emitted bytes match exactly; diagnostic detail, private notes, hidden-row metadata, and unapproved identifiers are structurally absent; and the recipient receives no workspace account, role grant, or query capability.

requirements.statement: A brief shall record the profile revisions used and become stale when relevant source information changes.
requirements.acceptance: Updating an attendee requirement marks the previous brief stale without rewriting its historical content.

requirements.statement: The product shall report recorded event participation counts per person, group, organisation, and time range.
requirements.acceptance: Counts link to the included events and exclude cancelled participation according to an inspectable policy.

requirements.statement: Access-log import shall provide source-specific column mapping, sample preview, validation, idempotency, and result evidence.
requirements.acceptance: Re-importing an identical source produces zero new events.

requirements.statement: Unknown badge identities shall remain unresolved until an authorised operator confirms a mapping.
requirements.acceptance: The importer never chooses a person solely from a fuzzy name match.

requirements.statement: Access events shall support retention, deletion, and legal-hold policy by workspace and source.
requirements.acceptance: Expiry deletes eligible payloads and records evidence without retaining the deleted sensitive data.

requirements.statement: The core product shall not calculate productivity, performance, attendance-compliance, or employee-risk scores from access data.
requirements.acceptance: No application/API/AI query exposes such a score and policy tests reject plugin/provider claims that require raw access scope for scoring.

requirements.statement: Facilities summaries shall use bounded aggregates with minimum-group and role controls.
requirements.acceptance: A user without raw-event permission can view an authorised occupancy summary but cannot drill into individual events.

requirements.statement: Users shall create one-time or policy-bounded recurring relationship reminders from an allowed relationship context, source, and reason; B0 event follow-up remains an Events-owned type.
requirements.acceptance: Completion history remains available, recurrence outside the allowlist is rejected, and no reminder becomes a generic task, project, or B0 event record.

requirements.statement: A relationship reminder shall explain why contact is due and provide a source-linked next action without claiming relationship quality.
requirements.acceptance: The personal review surface never shows an unexplained person name or score as the entire reminder and Workplace purpose rejects the feature.

requirements.statement: Users shall snooze, complete, skip, or change an allowed relationship cadence with undo where practical and with explicit source and context.
requirements.acceptance: Each action records actor, context, source, and result, preserves a recovery route, and cannot create assignees, checklists, dependencies, projects, workload, or boards.

requirements.statement: A provider shall implement a versioned capability contract and publish a descriptor before registration.
requirements.acceptance: Registration rejects an unknown contract version or a descriptor missing destinations, secret slots, limits, or safe modes.

requirements.statement: A connection shall be unable to access data until an active purpose-bound grant is approved.
requirements.acceptance: Provider tests show zero data calls before grant activation.

requirements.statement: A grant shall constrain provider, endpoint, operations, data classes or fields, record scope, schedule, retention, expiry, and approver.
requirements.acceptance: Changing any constrained value requires a new grant revision and disclosure preview.

requirements.statement: Every connection job shall be idempotent or explicitly report why the provider cannot guarantee idempotency.
requirements.acceptance: Retry tests do not duplicate immutable objects, imported records, or webhook effects under supported semantics.

requirements.statement: Provider UI and CLI labels shall distinguish backup, single-writer publication, transport, and multi-writer synchronisation.
requirements.acceptance: A provider cannot advertise a stronger mode than its current conformance evidence.

requirements.statement: Secrets shall be stored as opaque secret-store references and resolved only for an approved operation.
requirements.acceptance: Workspace, logs, exports, and provider descriptors contain no credential values.

requirements.statement: Connected-local egress shall reject undeclared destinations, redirects, operations, data scopes, and expired grants.
requirements.acceptance: A redirect and DNS-rebinding test cannot escape the approved destination policy.

requirements.statement: Backups shall be encrypted and validated locally before provider upload.
requirements.acceptance: Provider fixtures never receive plaintext canonical files and a corrupt local snapshot is not uploaded as successful.

requirements.statement: Backup success shall require an isolated restore verification, not upload completion alone.
requirements.acceptance: A missing or truncated remote chunk causes verification failure and leaves the current workspace untouched.

requirements.statement: Liaison shall act as an outbound CardDAV client to a user-chosen existing provider, synchronise only granted views or groups, and preserve unknown vCard properties; the first topology shall not expose a Liaison LAN CardDAV server.
requirements.acceptance: Named iOS and Android clients receive only the granted cohort through the chosen provider, a round trip retains unmodelled vCard fields, and listener inspection finds no Liaison CardDAV server.

requirements.statement: Calendar import shall preserve UID, recurrence identity, source revision, attendee mapping, and deletion policy.
requirements.acceptance: Recurring event refresh updates the correct occurrence without duplicating the series.

requirements.statement: Google Drive, WebDAV, S3-compatible, and local-folder adapters shall reuse provider-neutral object-store and backup contracts.
requirements.acceptance: No product workflow imports a provider SDK or provider-specific DTO.

requirements.statement: Remote transports shall exchange encrypted immutable operations and manifests rather than concurrently editing shared plaintext canonical files.
requirements.acceptance: Two devices reconcile through signed operations while each retains a readable local workspace.

requirements.statement: Shared operations shall verify workspace, actor, device, signature, chain, schema, authorisation, and base revision before materialisation.
requirements.acceptance: Invalid, replayed, revoked, out-of-order, and unauthorised operations are quarantined with evidence.

requirements.statement: Conflicts affecting the same invariant shall require an inspectable resolution rather than latest-timestamp wins.
requirements.acceptance: Concurrent edits to the same dietary coverage state create a conflict record with base, local, and incoming values.

requirements.statement: Private overlays shall be excluded from unauthorised materialisation, search, export, provider jobs, AI context, and existence disclosure.
requirements.acceptance: An unauthorised member cannot infer overlay content or existence through counts, errors, indexes, or exports.

requirements.statement: A self-service request shall identify requester, purpose, exact requested fields, expiry, disclosure policy, and response key.
requirements.acceptance: A recipient can provide, reduce, or decline information without creating an account.

requirements.statement: A Liaison Card shall contain only selected signed claims and an explicit update/revocation policy.
requirements.acceptance: Import never treats a card as authorisation to access the source workspace.

requirements.statement: Member and device revocation shall prevent future authorised operations and trigger applicable key rotation.
requirements.acceptance: A revoked device cannot materialise a new operation even when it retains an old signing key.

requirements.statement: The local API shall bind to loopback by default and require scoped, expiring, revocable credentials.
requirements.acceptance: A fresh installation exposes no unauthenticated listener and rejects a token outside its operation scope.

requirements.statement: Webhooks shall use destination grants, signatures, replay protection, idempotency keys, bounded retry, and delivery evidence.
requirements.acceptance: A replayed delivery and an unapproved redirect are rejected.

requirements.statement: MCP read tools shall return source references and effective grant information.
requirements.acceptance: A generated briefing can trace each returned relationship fact to an authorised source record.

requirements.statement: MCP and AI write tools shall create staged proposals by default.
requirements.acceptance: The user can inspect affected records, fields, old values, new values, provenance, and consequences before application.

requirements.statement: AI output shall pass normal domain validation and revision checks.
requirements.acceptance: A model cannot create an invalid dietary state or overwrite a newer profile revision.

requirements.statement: Liaison RM shall support an Ollama-compatible local inference path without a remote account.
requirements.acceptance: A complete approved local summarisation workflow runs with network denied.

requirements.statement: Remote AI disclosure shall require a named provider, endpoint, purpose, fields, records, operation, retention, and expiry grant.
requirements.acceptance: The disclosure preview matches the actual outbound payload in a deterministic fixture.

requirements.statement: Plugins shall use versioned WIT contracts and denied-by-default capabilities.
requirements.acceptance: A plugin cannot access filesystem paths, network destinations, secrets, or records absent from its approved manifest.

requirements.statement: Plugins shall not receive a raw database handle or unrestricted canonical workspace path.
requirements.acceptance: Host imports expose only context application commands, queries, and scoped content handles.

requirements.statement: Every supported task shall be completable by keyboard with visible focus and logical focus restoration.
requirements.acceptance: Automated and manual keyboard scripts complete each release UAT without pointer input.

requirements.statement: User-facing controls, state, errors, and status messages shall expose appropriate screen-reader semantics.
requirements.acceptance: Release evidence records the tested platform, screen reader, task, and unresolved defects.

requirements.statement: The interface shall support 400 percent zoom and reflow without loss of information or action on each declared supported desktop webview.
requirements.acceptance: Release UAT completes at 200 and 400 percent zoom without clipped content, horizontal task-flow scrolling, hidden focus, or unavailable action.

requirements.statement: The interface shall honour reduced-motion preferences and avoid motion required to understand state.
requirements.acceptance: All animated transitions disable or simplify under reduced motion while status remains perceivable.

requirements.statement: Long workflows shall preserve drafts and expose a clear resume point after interruption or restart.
requirements.acceptance: A user exits during event or profile editing and resumes without reconstructing completed steps.

requirements.statement: Every user-facing capability shall define empty, loading, partial, stale, conflict, permission, error, success, undo, and recovery states as applicable.
requirements.acceptance: The PR and UAT evidence identify each applicable state and its accessible announcement.

requirements.statement: Dashboard configuration and reordering shall have direct keyboard alternatives and restore-default behaviour.
requirements.acceptance: No drag operation is required to add, remove, move, or restore a panel.

requirements.statement: The product shall target WCAG 2.2 Level AA and applicable EN 301 549 requirements with recorded evidence.
requirements.acceptance: A release cannot claim the target met while a known critical or serious applicable defect remains undisclosed.

requirements.statement: The project shall publish installable Ubuntu/Flatpak, macOS, and Windows artifacts from reviewed source.
requirements.acceptance: Each artifact completes install, first run, workspace create/open, upgrade, and uninstall/reinstall smoke tests.

requirements.statement: Airgap and Connected-local artifacts shall be distinguishable in filename, application metadata, About screen, CLI version output, and provenance.
requirements.acceptance: A user and automated test can identify the profile without executing a network request.

requirements.statement: The Airgap Flatpak shall not request network permission.
requirements.acceptance: Manifest inspection and runtime tests show no network permission or successful socket access.

requirements.statement: Release artifacts shall include checksums, provenance, changelog, migration notes, known issues, and recovery knowledge.
requirements.acceptance: The release checklist links each artifact to its source commit and verification evidence.

requirements.statement: Uninstall shall not silently delete a user-selected workspace.
requirements.acceptance: Uninstall/reinstall tests preserve and reopen the workspace unless the user explicitly deletes it.

requirements.statement: CLI, desktop, imports, automation, and tests shall call one Rust application composition root with typed commands, results, and errors.
requirements.acceptance: Contract tests run the same workspace and Person commands through CLI and Tauri adapters without duplicating domain or persistence rules.

requirements.statement: An open workspace shall be represented by one session that owns the operating-system writer lock, repositories, recovery state, key state, projection state, and read-only degradation; file synchronisation or rename shall not transfer writer authority implicitly.
requirements.acceptance: A second process, renamed path, or file-sync copy receives a typed non-writer or identity error while read-only Health remains available, and only explicit close or process exit releases the validated lock.

requirements.statement: Every canonical mutation shall use a recoverable multi-target operation that stages and flushes every target, records original revision and digest preconditions, rechecks final per-file identity and digest, persists one durable commit decision, then rolls forward committed targets with progress evidence.
requirements.acceptance: Fault tests at every file and phase discard pre-commit staging or roll forward post-commit targets; rename, replacement, file-sync, stale revision, and non-cooperating external edits are detected and never overwritten.

requirements.statement: Command failures and Health findings shall use versioned structured envelopes with stable codes, recovery guidance, and correlation identifiers.
requirements.acceptance: CLI human/JSON and Tauri tests preserve the same code, details, recovery action, and command correlation without parsing error strings.

requirements.statement: Sensitive persistable values shall use authenticated sealed envelopes under a recoverable workspace key hierarchy and shall have no plaintext marker representation.
requirements.acceptance: Type and serialization tests cannot encode sensitive plaintext; tamper, wrong-passphrase, nonce, rotation, and clean-Keychain recovery tests pass.

requirements.statement: The local policy boundary shall identify one trusted workspace owner and device and enforce purpose-bound grants before sensitive access.
requirements.acceptance: Missing, expired, revoked, or out-of-scope grants expose no sensitive payload through results, errors, logs, audit, browser state, or projections.

requirements.statement: Directory reads shall tolerate malformed canonical siblings and expose bounded findings while healthy records remain searchable.
requirements.acceptance: A 10,000-person fixture containing malformed records still opens, paginates healthy results, and reports each invalid source through Health.

requirements.statement: Directory search and filtering shall use a disposable projection that revalidates canonical revisions before cohort finalization.
requirements.acceptance: Deleting or corrupting the projection causes a deterministic rebuild without semantic loss, and a stale projection cannot finalize a cohort.

requirements.statement: B0 dietary records shall contain only a constrained category and operational catering instruction and shall not collect diagnostic or medical narrative.
requirements.acceptance: Schemas, UI, exports, logs, audit, and fixtures have no field capable of carrying diagnosis, treatment, or medical history.

requirements.statement: Internal catering brief generation and external file delivery shall be separate evidenced operations.
requirements.acceptance: A failed or retried external delivery leaves one valid immutable internal brief, never overwrites an earlier export, and records verified delivery or retryable failure.

requirements.statement: Workplace Review shall support only EventReadinessFollowUp records bounded to an event, attendee, source gap, allowed resolution state, owner, due/expiry time, provenance, and completion evidence; it shall not implement a generic task engine, relationship allocation, cadence, or attention weighting.
requirements.acceptance: B0 schemas, commands, policies, UI, exports, and tests accept only the bounded follow-up fields and states and cannot store assignees beyond the trusted owner, checklists, projects, dependencies, workload, person allocation, relationship value, cadence, attention weight, or workplace review ranking.

requirements.statement: The product shall distinguish quiescent local checkpoints from user-portable encrypted recovery packages.
requirements.acceptance: UI, CLI, manifests, and evidence never label an unencrypted checkpoint as secure backup or clean-install recovery.

requirements.statement: An encrypted recovery package shall restore a B0 workspace on a clean installation without the original operating-system key cache.
requirements.acceptance: An isolated clean-Mac exercise restores canonical records and sealed values using the package and recovery passphrase while leaving the source workspace unchanged.

requirements.statement: The B0 desktop shall implement Overview, Directory, Events, Health, Settings, and the Event Details state machine through one semantic component and token system.
requirements.acceptance: Compiled-app keyboard, VoiceOver, contrast, 400 percent zoom and reflow, narrow-window, reduced-motion, interruption, and state coverage evidence passes.

requirements.statement: B0 acceptance shall be run against a freshly built and installed universal macOS review application.
requirements.acceptance: The installed application completes first launch through brief delivery, relaunch, offline use, readable-file inspection, and encrypted clean-install recovery with exact-head provenance.

requirements.statement: Personal Memory Alpha shall begin only after B0 acceptance and shall preserve the complete B0 regression matrix.
requirements.acceptance: A0 adds profile editing, interactions, commitments, reason-only Review, last-interaction context, open loops, purpose-bound workspace defaults, and supported appearance controls while every B0 gate remains green.

requirements.statement: Workspace-local people shall support multiple effective-dated relationship contexts within one purpose boundary; Personal, Family, Professional, Networking, and Workplace workspaces shall never silently share facts.
requirements.acceptance: A fixture with same-purpose contexts preserves effective dates while cross-purpose search, counts, exports, and attention rules remain isolated.

requirements.statement: The product shall export a versioned settings-only bundle containing layouts, fields, packs, templates, views, appearance, and policies but no records, secrets, tokens, caches, or device paths.
requirements.acceptance: An adversarial settings export contains no record identifiers, personal values, secret-like values, absolute paths, or cache data and validates against the settings schema.

requirements.statement: Settings import shall provide validation, migration and diff preview, conflict choices, dry-run, rollback, and unknown-safe-field preservation.
requirements.acceptance: A clean workspace previews and applies a compatible bundle, rejects unsafe literals without partial mutation, preserves unknown safe keys, and restores the prior settings revision on rollback.

requirements.statement: An optional cross-workspace identity bridge shall contain opaque references only, copy no facts, grant no access, remain excluded from ordinary export, and be safely removable.
requirements.acceptance: Adding and removing a bridge changes no profile value, search result, count, export, assessment, or attention policy across purpose boundaries.

requirements.statement: Deletion shall propagate through canonical history, backup expiry, purge or compaction, and restore-time deletion replay so verified restore cannot silently resurrect purged sensitive data.
requirements.acceptance: Restoring a pre-deletion snapshot in isolation replays authenticated deletion records and neither payload nor identifying metadata becomes active or searchable.

requirements.statement: One typed People authoring adapter shall own strict writes to the pinned OKF v0.1 Draft profile and tolerant reads. Legacy normalization shall be previewable, backup-first, journaled, failure-atomic, idempotent, exactly reversible, and preserve body bytes, unknown extensions, links, malformed siblings, and manually authored reserved or index content.
requirements.acceptance: Fault injection at every normalization write boundary either discards pre-commit staging or recovers committed work; restart, rerun and exact rollback preserve stable IDs, unknown keys and sections, Markdown body bytes, ordinary links, curated indexes, malformed siblings and healthy People without expanding P01 or P02.

requirements.statement: A person shall support optional purpose-scoped Working With Me sections and clearly separate Shared Guide from Private Interaction Notes.
requirements.acceptance: Sharing or exporting the guide never includes private observations, their count, or their existence.

requirements.statement: Working With Me sections shall carry authorship, approval hash, consent, source, freshness, sharing scope, sensitivity, expiry, correction, withdrawal, and purge behaviour.
requirements.acceptance: Create, correct, expire, withdraw, and purge operations preserve provenance and do not overstate consent or authorship.

requirements.statement: Users shall reorder, rename, pin, hide, and conditionally promote profile tabs and sections without changing stable IDs or hiding canonical content from non-visual access and export.
requirements.acceptance: Keyboard and screen-reader workflows round-trip two layouts while CLI, canonical values, validation, and export semantics remain unchanged.

requirements.statement: Profile photos shall be local assets with provenance, accessible presentation, export, correction, and deletion.
requirements.acceptance: A photo can be replaced and purged without changing person identity, and every rendered photo has an appropriate accessible name or decorative treatment.

requirements.statement: Working With Me shall distinguish owner observation, subject-supplied, locally recorded subject confirmation, and signature-verified self-authorship without overstating identity proof.
requirements.acceptance: Unsigned, locally witnessed, and valid signed fixtures receive distinct accurate labels and invalid signatures cannot become verified authorship.

requirements.statement: Every Liaison-authored non-reserved People Markdown document shall conform to the pinned OKF v0.1 Draft envelope on write with a non-empty `type: person` and mapped core fields; Liaison's versioned domain fields remain the schema authority through namespaced extensions and sealed sensitive values shall never enter plaintext for OKF conformance.
requirements.acceptance: Every B0 writer emits the pinned OKF envelope and stable Liaison extension, validates the domain schema separately, preserves an ordinary Markdown-tool round trip, and leak scans prove sealed dietary or other sensitive facts never appear in frontmatter, body, indexes, errors, logs, or projections.

requirements.statement: OKF conformance shall not imply Liaison domain validity: unsupported or invalid domain facts remain inert or quarantined and cannot affect event readiness; tolerant reads preserve unknown types, keys, links, and sections without blocking healthy People.
requirements.acceptance: Missing optional OKF fields, unknown document types and keys, ordinary broken links, malformed siblings, stale revisions, invalid purposes, unauthorised disclosure and unsealed sensitive values yield bounded findings or quarantine while healthy People remain available and no invalid fact reaches readiness.

requirements.statement: A source-complete purpose-scoped profile shall compose identity and contact context, effective memberships, dates, Working With Me, distinct last-interaction and last-note summaries, manual assessment, derived freshness, timeline, related People, and unresolved source or identity warnings into an accessible overview and stable configurable tabs; no global person score shall exist.
requirements.acceptance: The installed A0 profile exposes each named section through semantic navigation and source links, preserves purpose boundaries and distinct summaries after reopen, and files, CLI, UI, projection, settings, exports and adapters contain no global person score.

requirements.statement: Every profile claim shall expose canonical source, authorship, recorded and confirmed dates, information state, purpose, disclosure or sensitivity, and correction history without implying that missing provider coverage means no interaction.
requirements.acceptance: A user can inspect the complete evidence drawer by keyboard and screen reader, and missing or bounded provider coverage is labelled with requested and covered ranges rather than converted to a negative fact.

requirements.statement: Conflicting, stale, withheld, and imported-proposal facts shall remain distinct prominent profile states; Liaison shall never silently select a winner or convert an observation into subject-authored content.
requirements.acceptance: Fixtures for every state remain visually and semantically distinct, require explicit correction or confirmation, preserve all sources, and survive file round trip without an automatic winner.

requirements.statement: External edits to controlled profile facts shall produce stable-ID field diffs or staged proposals with explicit accept, reject, or correct actions; unknown safe material shall survive and no external edit shall silently change disclosure or operational readiness.
requirements.acceptance: External-edit fixtures show exact before and after values plus sources, preserve unknown safe material, require a human decision, recover after interruption, and leave disclosure and event readiness unchanged until a valid domain command commits.

requirements.statement: Relationship assessment and contact freshness shall remain independent; assessment is user-authored while freshness is reproducibly derived from visible cadence and qualifying interactions.
requirements.acceptance: Changing assessment does not alter freshness, and every freshness value links to its interaction inputs, cadence, clock, and rule version.

requirements.statement: In self-owned non-Workplace workspaces only, versioned relationship-context allocation policies shall be transparent, previewable, suppression-aware, and prohibited from human-worth or employee-performance use; Workplace schemas and surfaces shall deny them and no global person weight shall exist.
requirements.acceptance: A personal preview explains every term and suppression while Workplace files, CLI, UI, settings, exports, and adapter fixtures reject the capability and value.

requirements.statement: Every attention reason shall expose inputs, source record IDs, units or formula, policy or rule version, plain-language explanation, and corrective, dismiss, or snooze actions.
requirements.acceptance: A reason-only queue can be reproduced from visible sources and the user can correct, dismiss, or snooze it without changing relationship assessment.

requirements.statement: Notes and general journal entries shall be first-class canonical records with Markdown round trip, links, timeline presentation, and pin or promotion metadata without copied content.
requirements.acceptance: A pinned externally edited note reopens once, retains unknown Markdown, and appears by reference rather than duplicated body content.

requirements.statement: Configurable relationship-review and message templates shall remain local and user-triggered and shall not create mass outreach without a separate explicit grant.
requirements.acceptance: Applying a template creates one reviewed local draft and cannot enumerate or contact a cohort.

requirements.statement: On-device voice capture shall create a source-preserving staged interaction proposal and work with networking denied.
requirements.acceptance: A denied-network fixture retains the local source, previews the transcript and structured mapping, and persists nothing before confirmation.

requirements.statement: A0 relationship reminders shall require a relationship context or source and allowed reason, use the bounded state machine, restrict recurrence, and expose no general task or project fields; B0 uses EventReadinessFollowUp instead.
requirements.acceptance: Allowed reminder transitions pass while assignee, checklist, dependency, project, workload, board, and arbitrary recurrence fields are rejected in files, CLI, UI, and later API fixtures.

requirements.statement: The connector-neutral relationship-follow-up handoff envelope and read-only external-status rules shall be specified in A0; concrete Meitheal or calendar connectors remain R6.
requirements.acceptance: A local fixture serializes a scoped handoff and ignores unauthorised external status fields without requiring or invoking a provider.

requirements.statement: Fast search, filters, and saved views shall expose predicates, respect grants, round-trip through settings, and support complete keyboard and screen-reader operation.
requirements.acceptance: A mixed professional saved view reproduces its predicates after clean settings import and excludes ungranted records in UI, CLI, and export.

requirements.statement: Each released UI shall name its supported operating-system, browser-webview, and assistive-technology matrix and scope accessibility claims to those shipped surfaces.
requirements.acceptance: Release evidence names exact versions, workflow coverage, known defects, and unsupported surfaces without claiming certification.

requirements.statement: One versioned semantic-token and component contract shall power system, light, dark, and high_contrast built-ins; every built-in shall preserve meaning and pass the complete B0 accessibility matrix before B0 acceptance.
requirements.acceptance: The installed Details to Cohort to Attendees to Readiness to Brief journey passes contrast, focus, non-colour status, reflow, target, reduced-motion, restart, and recovery evidence in every built-in.

requirements.statement: Appearance settings shall provide previewable density, text scale, motion, and validated palette choices with reset, persistence, keyboard and screen-reader operation, and classified settings export or import without record data.
requirements.acceptance: A clean-workspace round trip preserves supported and unknown-safe settings, leaks no record data, and causes no semantic or canonical-value loss.

requirements.statement: Declarative theme packages shall use a versioned, checksummed, licence-aware manifest and semantic tokens only; executable code, arbitrary CSS, remote assets, filesystem or network capability, and activation without validation or rollback shall be rejected.
requirements.acceptance: A compatible local package previews and rolls back, while incompatible, corrupt, code-bearing, remote-asset, and malicious fixtures leave the active built-in unchanged.

requirements.statement: Optional spatial discovery shall provide coarse-location controls, explicit geocoding egress preview and grant, local or no-network behaviour, coordinate correction and deletion, structural Workplace denial, and an equivalent semantic list or table; it shall never rank People by a relationship score.
requirements.acceptance: Denied-provider and explicitly granted fixtures return equivalent semantic results, expose and revoke egress, allow coordinate correction and deletion, deny the capability in Workplace schemas and surfaces, and contain no hidden geocoding, global person score, ranking, or colour-only result.

requirements.statement: Event readiness shall store availability, freshness, conflict, and disclosure separately and derive exactly one outcome through an ordered, versioned, fail-closed decision table.
requirements.acceptance: Property tests cover every supported and invalid combination in order and persist the decision-table version without overwriting source facts.

requirements.statement: The attendee lifecycle shall handle duplicate, unresolved identity, invitation, confirmation, cancellation, removal, walk-in, attendance, no-show, and event cancellation with exact denominator reconciliation and superseding corrections.
requirements.acceptance: Transition and property tests reconcile the active deduplicated denominator after every allowed transition and keep invalid, historical, and corrected rows inspectable.

requirements.statement: Real workplace-data pilots shall be blocked until data-controller, purpose and legal-basis, sensitive-data condition, DPIA decision, notice, retention, rights, incident, and independent legal-review responsibilities are recorded.
requirements.acceptance: Synthetic UAT remains available, but a real-data mode or import is denied until the dated governance checklist and independent reviewer evidence are active.

requirements.statement: B0 shall have one trusted workspace owner; recipients shall never access the workspace and shall receive only immutable, purpose-bound, expiring, audited least-disclosure brief snapshots whose emitted bytes match preview.
requirements.acceptance: Brief evidence records input revisions, creator, recipient, purpose, expiry, field policy, checksum, audit receipt, exact preview and emitted-byte equality, and structural absence of names or restricted details unless explicitly required by the approved policy.

requirements.statement: Meerkat, Monica, and CRM-in-Markdown migrations shall provide preview, identity reconciliation, provenance, unsupported-data reporting, rollback, and idempotent re-import.
requirements.acceptance: Each synthetic source produces a deterministic reconciliation report and a second import creates no duplicate person, relationship, note, activity, reminder, or custom field.

requirements.statement: Local and Airgap-safe business-card OCR shall preserve source imagery, preview mapping and duplicates, and require confirmation before persistence.
requirements.acceptance: With networking denied, the operator can inspect and correct the proposal, confirm selected fields, and retain or delete the source under policy.

requirements.statement: Every provider or import operation shall expose connector and version, grant, requested source and range, last attempt and success, created, updated, skipped and quarantined counts, conflicts and errors, retry, revoke, retention, and history in an operations feed separate from relationship reminders; hidden refresh and unreported egress are prohibited.
requirements.acceptance: A migration or provider fixture records complete operation receipts, bounded source coverage, failures and retries, then revocation stops all refresh and egress; no receipt, status, count or error creates or mutates a relationship reminder.

requirements.statement: Goal-based network discovery shall be source-linked and grant-scoped and shall not infer closeness, quality, loyalty, employee value, or likelihood.
requirements.acceptance: Every suggestion links to authorised factual sources and prohibited inference fixtures are rejected before display.

requirements.statement: Packaged n8n examples shall use only versioned scoped API and webhook contracts and include allow, deny, revoke, retry, and idempotence evidence.
requirements.acceptance: The example succeeds within scope and fails closed after revocation, on redirect, on replay, and outside its operation or record scope.

requirements.statement: Relationship follow-ups may be handed to Meitheal or another execution system through a grant; Liaison shall not implement general task assignment, dependencies, routines, or execution.
requirements.acceptance: The handoff and permitted read-only status mirror pass while Liaison schemas, UI, CLI, API, MCP, and plugins expose no general project or workload surface.

requirements.statement: Life-change, biography, and enrichment detection shall create only a source-backed staged proposal containing old and new values, source, confidence, egress context, and explicit review; it shall never directly overwrite a confirmed fact or automatically change assessment, freshness, or cadence.
requirements.acceptance: Review, reject, correct and accept flows pass through application commands, while AI, MCP, plugins, providers and import adapters cannot directly write a confirmed fact or mutate assessment, freshness, cadence, disclosure, or operational readiness.

requirements.statement: Public documentation shall label each material capability shipped, beta, planned, marketed externally, or out and link every shipped claim to release evidence.
requirements.acceptance: A generated claim inventory rejects a shipped label without exact-head evidence and identifies external comparator evidence as external.

requirements.statement: Public releases shall include install, recovery, migration, accessibility, privacy and security, support, and contribution paths suitable for non-developer evaluation.
requirements.acceptance: A clean-machine evaluator can find and complete the applicable paths without private project context.

requirements.statement: A generated normative appendix shall trace every requirement and UAT case to exactly one owning gate, implementation task, evidence owner, milestone, and current status without orphan or cross-release false claim.
requirements.acceptance: Repository validation regenerates byte-identical JSON and Markdown reports and fails on any missing, duplicate, unknown, contradictory, stale, or out-of-order ownership edge.
